critical_format_rules: NEVER use placeholder text like [Name], [Date], _____, XXXX, etc.
Use actual names, dates, amounts from the instructions provided
If exact date unknown, use 'on or about [month/year]' or describe contextually
If name unknown, use role descriptors ('the plaintiff', 'the defendant')
Document must be complete and ready to file - NO blanks
Output CLEAN MARKDOWN only — no HTML tags, no code fences
Use markdown pipe tables with |---| separator for ALL tabular data
Use **bold** for names and emphasis, ## headings for sections

legal_notice.description: Legal notices for demands, complaints, breach of trust, cheque bounce, eviction, etc.

structure.header: Advocate name, credentials (B.Com. LL.B., etc.), office address, contact details
structure.recipient_section:
**To,**
1. **Recipient Name**
   Full Address
   Contact Number

2. Second Recipient (if any)...
structure.subject: **SUBJECT:** Notice for [specific issue] caused by you to my client **[Client Name]**
structure.opening: Under instructions from and on behalf of my client **[Client Name]**, [Occupation], R/O [Full Address], I am sending this legal notice to you as under –
structure.body: Numbered paragraphs (1, 2, 3...) with chronological facts. Each paragraph starts with 'That...' and states specific facts with dates, amounts, and details.
structure.legal_grounds: State applicable sections of law (IPC Section 406, 420 / BNS equivalents, NI Act Section 138, etc.)
structure.demand_clause: Within **[X] days** from the receipt of this notice, you are called upon to [specific demand - pay amount, return property, cease action, etc.]
structure.consequence: Failing which, my client shall be constrained to initiate appropriate civil and criminal proceedings against you...
structure.closing:
Issued under my signatures on this [date].

**Advocate for the Noticee**
**[Advocate Name]**
structure.copy_to: **Copy to:-** My client for information and record.

format_guidelines: Use formal legal language - firm but professional tone
All amounts in words and figures: Rs. 80,000/- (Rupees Eighty Thousand Only)
Include specific dates for all events
Number all paragraphs sequentially
State legal provisions clearly (Section 406, 420 of IPC / BNS)
Give reasonable time limit (typically 7-15 days)
End with clear consequences of non-compliance
Output clean MARKDOWN — no HTML tags
legal_notice.example_snippet:
**SUBJECT:** Notice for Criminal Breach of Trust, Cheating and Mental Harassment caused by you to my client **Dr. Mahavir Bhagwandas Gandhi**

---

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client **Dr. Mahavir Bhagwandas Gandhi**, R/O 49 Samta Colony, Vinayak Nagar, Nagpur, I am sending this legal notice to you as under –

1. That my client is a Medical Practitioner and was running a clinic...

2. That the Noticee No. 1 approached my client in year 2022...

15. Within **7 days** from the receipt of this notice, you are called upon to refund Rs. 80,000/- (Rupees Eighty Thousand Only) along with interest...

I, [Full Name], Age: XX yrs, Occ: [Occupation], the Plaintiff in above matter, residing at [Address], do hereby state on solemn affirmation that what is stated in the above paragraphs no. 1 to [X] are true & correct to the best of my knowledge & information, which I believe to be true. Hence verified at [City] on this ___ day of ______YYYY.

Place: [City]
Date: ____/____/YYYY

Plaintiff

I know the Deponent.

Advocate for the Plaintiff

format_guidelines: Use 'I say that...' or 'That...' to begin each paragraph
State facts chronologically
Include specific dates, amounts, and addresses
Reference any prior legal notices or communications
State cause of action clearly
Include jurisdiction statement
End with verification on oath
Output clean MARKDOWN — no HTML tags

I, Shri Jagdish Panditrao Chaudhari, Age 50 years, residing at 41, Mahaveer Nagar, Part 2, Darwha Road, Yavatmal, Maharashtra - 445001, the plaintiff, do hereby state on solemn affirmation as under:

1. I say that I am the owner of the property situated at flat no. 426 measuring about 717 sq ft, in Lotus Nandanvan society, Moshi, Pimpri Chinchwad, Pune...

2. That the defendant was in need of a flat and therefore inquired with the security guard...

structure.title: ## Affidavit For Interim Application
structure.opening: I, [Title] [Full Name], Age [XX] years, residing at [Full Address], the plaintiff, do hereby state on solemn affirmation as under:
structure.body: Numbered paragraphs (1, 2, 3...) with facts - each starting with 'I say that' or 'That'
structure.urgency: State urgency, bonafide requirement, and irreparable harm
structure.prayer_closing: **AND FOR WHICH ACT OF KINDNESS AND JUSTICE, THE PLAINTIFF SHALL AS IN DUTY BOUND EVER PRAY.**
structure.signature_block:
Place: [City]    Plaintiff    Advocate for the Plaintiff
Date: ____/____/YYYY        [Advocate Name]
structure.verification:
## VERIFICATION

Full verification paragraph
Place/Date + Plaintiff signature + 'I know the deponent.' + Advocate signature

format_guidelines: PARTY BLOCKS: Name in **bold**, details on separate lines, role marker (………Plaintiff) at end
ADDRESS FORMAT: Break into multiple lines - Street, Area, City, State - Pincode
NUMBERED PARAGRAPHS: Start with 'I say that' for first paragraph, 'That' for subsequent
AMOUNTS: Always write in figures AND words: Rs. 4,25,000/- (Rupees Four Lakh Twenty Five Thousand Only)
DATES: Use DD/MM/YYYY format for specific dates, 'on or about [month/year]' for approximate
Output clean MARKDOWN — no HTML tags
content_requirements: Para 1: State ownership/relationship to suit property
Para 2-N: Chronological facts of how dispute arose
State all communication attempts with defendant
Mention any legal notices sent/received with dates
State bonafide requirement with specific reasons (family need, medical, education)
State irreparable harm if relief not granted
State cause of action and when it arose
State jurisdiction
State amounts due with calculation (months x rent = total)
Final paragraph: Solemn affirmation of truth
interim_application.example_full_document:
# IN THE HON'BLE SMALL CAUSES COURT PUNE AT PUNE

**Civil Suit No.______ /2022**

**Jagdish Panditrao Choudhari**
Age: 50 yrs, Occ: Manager Mechanical
Raymonds Ltd. (Denim Division),
R/o: 41, Mahaveer Nagar, Part 2, Darwha
Road, Yavatmal, Maharashtra - 445001
Mob. no. 9373188011        ………Plaintiff

**Vs.**

**Omprakash Keshavrao Devgavdkar**
Age: 50 yrs, Occ: Business,
R/o: Gate no. 274, Lotus Nandanvan Apartment,
Flat no. 426, B.no. 01, Moshi,
District Pune - 412105
Mob. no. 9769205996        …….Defendant

---

## Affidavit For Interim Application

I, Shri Jagdish Panditrao Chaudhari, Age 50 years, residing at 41, Mahaveer Nagar, Part 2, Darwha Road, Yavatmal, Maharashtra - 445001, the plaintiff, do hereby state on solemn affirmation as under:

1. I say that I am the owner of the property situated at flat no. 426 measuring about 717 sq ft, in Lotus Nandanvan society, Moshi, Pimpri Chinchwad, Pune and I am currently residing at 41, Mahaveer Nagar, Part 2, Darwha Road, Yavatmal. I am filing the present affidavit for the purpose of stating the facts in the said application.

2. That the defendant was in need of a flat and therefore inquired with the security guard of the Lotus Nandanvan society, and was informed by the security guard of the Lotus Nandanvan society about my flat.

3. That the defendant approached me and requested me to provide the suit premises on leave and license basis till 04/01/2022, to which I agreed.

4. That the defendant inquired with me for the license fee for the suit property, to which I had informed about the deposit of Rs. 20,000/- (Rupees Twenty Thousand Only) and a monthly license fee of Rs. 8,500/- (Rupees Eight Thousand Five Hundred Only). That the defendant agreed to pay the deposit of Rs. 20,000/- to me by cash, and a monthly fee of Rs. 8,500/-.

22. That I do hereby state on solemn affirmation that whatever is stated in the above paragraphs is true and correct to the best of my knowledge.

**AND FOR WHICH ACT OF KINDNESS AND JUSTICE, THE PLAINTIFF SHALL AS IN DUTY BOUND EVER PRAY.**

---

Place: Pune      Plaintiff      Advocate for the Plaintiff
Date: ____/____/2022          Sattyajeet S. Karale Patil

---

## VERIFICATION

I, Mr. Jagdish Panditrao Choudhari, Age: 50 yrs, Occ: Manager Mechanical Raymonds Ltd. (Denim Division), R/o: 41, Mahaveer Nagar, Part 2, Darwha Road, Yavatmal, Maharashtra - 445001, do hereby state on solemn affirmation that what is stated in the above paragraphs no. 1 to 22 are true & correct to the best of my knowledge & information, which I believe to be true. Hence verified at Pune on this ___ day of ______2022.

Place: Pune
Date: ____ /___/2022        Plaintiff

I know the deponent.

Advocate for the Plaintiff

structure.title: ## SUIT FOR POSSESSION UNDER SECTION [X] OF THE [ACT NAME]
structure.body: Numbered paragraphs (1, 2, 3...) starting with 'The plaintiff submits that...'
structure.cause_of_action: Paragraphs explaining when and how cause of action arose
structure.jurisdiction: The present court has jurisdiction to try this matter as the suit property is situated within the premises of the territorial jurisdiction of this court.
structure.valuation: The plaintiff is seeking for possession and hence the suit is valued at Rs. ________/-, hence court fee of amount Rs. ________/- has been paid.
structure.prayer:
The plaintiff therefore prays that,
a. [First relief sought]
b. [Second relief sought]
c. [Third relief sought]
d. Any just and equitable orders be passed in favour of the plaintiff in the interest of justice.

format_guidelines: Use 'The plaintiff submits that...' for each paragraph
State property details precisely (address, area, survey numbers)
Include chronological facts of tenancy/license
State defaults and breaches clearly
Mention any prior notices sent
Include bonafide requirement if applicable
State cause of action with specific dates
Prayer should have specific reliefs (a, b, c, d format)
Output clean MARKDOWN — no HTML tags
possession_suit.example_snippet:
## SUIT FOR POSSESSION UNDER SECTION 26 OF THE PROVINCIAL SMALL CAUSE COURTS ACT, 1887

1. The plaintiff submits that he is the owner of the property situated at flat no. 426 measuring about 717 sqft, in Lotus Nandanvan society, Moshi, Pimpri Chinchwad, Pune...

23. The plaintiff therefore prays that,
a. The defendant be ordered to pay the license fee amount of Rs. 4,08,000/- (Rupees Four Lakh Eight Thousand Only)
b. The defendant be ordered to pay Rs. 5,00,000/- (Rupees Five Lakh Only) for the inconvenience caused...

bail_application.description: Bail applications (regular, anticipatory, default) for criminal cases

structure.parties:
**[Full Name]** S/o Shri [Father], Age, Address ………Applicant

**Vs**

State of [State] through [Police Station], District [District] ………Non-Applicant
structure.crime_table:
| Description of Crime | Details of Impugned Order |
|----------------------|--------------------------|
| Crime No. | B.A. No. |
| U/s [Sections] | Name of Judge |
| PS [Name], Distt. | Name of Court |
| Date of arrest | Date of order |
structure.facts: **Facts of case :-** Numbered paragraphs with chronological facts
structure.grounds: **GROUNDS** with Roman numerals (I), (II), (III)... citing legal arguments and case laws
structure.prayer:
## PRAYER
Prayer for grant of bail with specific terms

format_guidelines: Use MCRC (Miscellaneous Criminal Case) format for High Court bail applications
All tables must use markdown pipe syntax with |---| separator rows
Roman numerals (I, II, III...) for GROUNDS section
Cite BOTH old law (CrPC/IPC) and new law (BNSS/BNS) section references
Include relevant Supreme Court judgments in grounds (Arnesh Kumar, Sanjay Chandra, etc.)
Mark '**(Applicant in Jail)**' prominently if applicant is in custody
State co-accused bail status if co-accused have been granted bail
Output clean MARKDOWN — no HTML tags
content_requirements: FIR/Crime details with all section numbers
Date of arrest and period of custody
Prior bail history (applications and outcomes)
Criminal antecedents (or state clean record)
Co-accused bail status
Brief facts from applicant's perspective
At least 8-10 grounds with legal citations
Clear prayer with specific relief sought
bail_application.example_snippet:
**MCRC No._______ / 2025**
**(Under Section 439 of CrPC / Section 483 of BNSS)**

**(Applicant in Jail)**

**Ramesh Kumar** S/o Shri Mohan Lal
Age: 32 years, R/o Village Barkheda,
Tehsil Harda, District Harda (M.P.)      ………Applicant

**Vs**

State of Madhya Pradesh
Through P.S. Harda, District Harda      ………Non-Applicant

---

| Description of Crime | Details |
|----------------------|---------|
| Crime No. 142/2025 | PS Harda |
| U/s 303, 34 BNS | FIR Date: 15/01/2025 |

---

**GROUNDS:**

(I) That the applicant is innocent and has been falsely implicated in the present case.

(II) That the investigation is complete and chargesheet has been filed. The applicant is no longer required for investigation.

(III) That the applicant has deep roots in the community and is not a flight risk.

---

## PRAYER

It is therefore most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to grant regular bail to the applicant in Crime No. 142/2025 registered at P.S. Harda, District Harda.

hindi.example_snippet:
**म.अ.प्र. क्रमांक _______ / 2025**
**(धारा 439 दण्ड प्रक्रिया संहिता / धारा 483 भारतीय नागरिक सुरक्षा संहिता के अंतर्गत)**

**(आवेदक जेल में है)**

**रमेश कुमार** पुत्र श्री मोहन लाल
आयु: 32 वर्ष, निवासी ग्राम बरखेड़ा,
तहसील हरदा, जिला हरदा (म.प्र.)      ………आवेदक

**विरुद्ध**

मध्यप्रदेश शासन
थाना हरदा, जिला हरदा द्वारा      ………अनावेदक

---

| अपराध क्रमांक | थाना | धाराएं | प्राथमिकी दिनांक |
|------------|------|--------|----------------|
| 142/2025 | थाना हरदा | 303, 34 भा.न्या.सं. | 15/01/2025 |

---

**आधार:**

(I) यह कि आवेदक निर्दोष है तथा उसे प्रस्तुत प्रकरण में झूठा फंसाया गया है।

(II) यह कि विवेचना पूर्ण हो चुकी है एवं आरोप पत्र दाखिल किया जा चुका है। आवेदक की विवेचना हेतु आवश्यकता नहीं है।

(III) यह कि आवेदक की समाज में गहरी जड़ें हैं तथा वह फरार होने की स्थिति में नहीं है।

---

## प्रार्थना

अतः माननीय न्यायालय से सविनय प्रार्थना है कि आवेदक को अपराध क्रमांक 142/2025 थाना हरदा, जिला हरदा में नियमित जमानत प्रदान की जावे।

criminal_appeal.description: Criminal appeals against conviction, sentence, or acquittal

structure.case_title:
**CRIMINAL APPEAL No. _______ / YYYY**
**(Under Section 374 CrPC / Section 415 BNSS)**
structure.parties:
**[Full Name]** S/o, Age, Address ………Appellant (अपीलार्थी)

**Vs**

State of [State] through [Prosecuting Agency] ………Respondent (प्रत्यर्थी)
structure.impugned_judgment_table:
| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Court | [Trial Court] |
| Case No. | [Sessions Trial No.] |
| Crime No. | [Crime No.], PS [Name] |
| Judgment Date | [DD/MM/YYYY] |
| Conviction | Under Section [X] of [Act] |
| Sentence | [X] years RI + Fine Rs. [Amount] |
structure.brief_facts: Numbered paragraphs: FIR details, prosecution case, trial proceedings, evidence, defence, trial court findings
structure.grounds:
## GROUNDS OF APPEAL
Roman numerals (I), (II), (III)... challenging trial court findings
structure.prayer:
## PRAYER
Prayer to allow appeal, set aside conviction, acquit or reduce sentence

format_guidelines: Reference BOTH old (CrPC/IPC) and new (BNSS/BNS) statutory provisions
Use Roman numerals for grounds of appeal
Cite Supreme Court and High Court judgments on appellate review standards
Tables must use markdown pipe syntax with |---| separator rows
Distinguish between appeal against conviction vs. appeal against sentence
For SC/ST Act, NDPS, POCSO cases: cite specific provisions of those statutes
Output clean MARKDOWN — no HTML tags
content_requirements: Complete details of impugned judgment (court, case no., date, conviction, sentence)
FIR/Crime details with sections
Brief chronological facts of prosecution case
Summary of evidence led at trial
Defence version and trial court findings
At least 8-10 substantive grounds of appeal
Relevant case law citations
Clear prayer (acquittal or reduction of sentence)
criminal_appeal.example_snippet:
**CRIMINAL APPEAL No. _______ / 2025**
**(Under Section 374 CrPC / Section 415 BNSS)**

**Ramesh Kumar** S/o Shri Mohan Lal
Age: 45 years, R/o Village Barkheda,
District Harda (M.P.)      ………Appellant

**Vs**

State of Madhya Pradesh      ………Respondent

---

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Court | Sessions Court, Harda |
| Case No. | ST 50/2024 |
| Crime No. | 89/2023, PS Harda |
| Judgment Date | 10/12/2024 |
| Conviction | Sec. 302 BNS |
| Sentence | Life Imprisonment |

---

## GROUNDS OF APPEAL

(I) That the impugned judgment of conviction is against the weight of evidence on record.

(II) That the learned trial court failed to appreciate the material contradictions in the statements of prosecution witnesses.

(III) That the prosecution has failed to establish the chain of circumstances beyond reasonable doubt.

hindi.example_snippet:
**आपराधिक अपील क्रमांक _______ / 2025**
**(धारा 374 दण्ड प्रक्रिया संहिता / धारा 415 भारतीय नागरिक सुरक्षा संहिता के अंतर्गत)**

**रमेश कुमार** पुत्र श्री मोहन लाल
आयु: 45 वर्ष, निवासी ग्राम बरखेड़ा,
जिला हरदा (म.प्र.)      ………अपीलार्थी

**विरुद्ध**

मध्यप्रदेश शासन      ………प्रत्यर्थी

---

| न्यायालय | प्रकरण क्रमांक | अपराध क्रमांक | निर्णय दिनांक | दोषसिद्धि | दण्डादेश |
|---------|--------------|-------------|-------------|----------|--------|
| सत्र न्यायालय, हरदा | एस.टी. 50/2024 | 89/2023, थाना हरदा | 10/12/2024 | धारा 302 भा.न्या.सं. | आजीवन कारावास |

---

## अपील के आधार

(I) यह कि दोषसिद्धि का विवादित निर्णय अभिलेख पर उपलब्ध साक्ष्य के विरुद्ध है।

(II) यह कि विद्वान विचारण न्यायालय ने अभियोजन साक्षियों के कथनों में महत्वपूर्ण विरोधाभासों की सराहना करने में त्रुटि की है।

(III) यह कि अभियोजन परिस्थितियों की श्रृंखला को युक्तियुक्त संदेह से परे स्थापित करने में विफल रहा है।

---

## प्रार्थना

अतः माननीय न्यायालय से सविनय प्रार्थना है कि:
(अ) प्रस्तुत अपील स्वीकार की जावे;
(ब) सत्र न्यायालय, हरदा द्वारा एस.टी. 50/2024 में पारित दिनांक 10/12/2024 का दोषसिद्धि आदेश अपास्त किया जावे;
(स) अपीलार्थी को समस्त आरोपों से दोषमुक्त किया जावे।
